title: Watches
text: Watches Watches are designated fields, variables, expressions, and functions that are continuously monitored while the application or component is debugged. You can set a watch for: Fields—all fields, whether defined in the application model or only in a component. Specify fully qualified fields (field and entity) to avoid confusion with local variables that may have the same name. Variables—all variables, including local, component, global, and general purpose variables ( $1 through $99 ). Expressions—any expression that the Uniface expression evaluator understands. If an expression is out of scope, for example, when a variable or field used in the expression is not in the current trigger, operation, or local ProcScript module, unexpected values can occur, or the expression can appear as undefined (a ProcScript error). Functions—all Uniface functions. Note: The debugger does not allow you to change the value of a ProcScript function. $status and $procerror are automatically included in the Watches window because of their importance in debugging an application. These functions provide information on the results of the previously executed ProcScript statement. CAUTION: Avoid setting a ProcScript function call as a watch. If a ProcScript function call is set as a watch, it is executed for every step as you step through code in the debugger. This can implicitly change the values of $status with each step, which can affect the ProcScript that is subsequently executed. Other variables may also be set, including $result , $procerror , $procerrorcontext , and $procreturncontext . Named constants, such as component and global constants, cannot be watched. At compile time, Uniface replaces all constant names with the actual expression value of that constant. Uniface debugging is done at deployment time (on compiled components), so the constant names are no longer available. Watches constitute part of the debugger state. Related Links Working with watches Add watches from the debugger main window or from the browser. Updating fields, variables, or watch values Update watch fields, variables, or values. Debugger State The state of the Debugger consists of current settings, watches, and breakpoints. The current state can be saved as a named configuration for repeated use. Variables Variables are placeholders for data whose value can change during the runtime duration of the ProcScript module, component, or application for which they have scope. The scope is determined by where they are declared. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
